title: Monetary Policy Report, February 25, 2022

source.name: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

summary: The Federal Reserve's February 2022 Monetary Policy Report reaffirms the FOMC's dual mandate of maximum employment and 2% inflation, outlines the flexible average inflation targeting framework, and documents economic conditions as of late February 2022. The report was prepared as Russia launched its invasion of Ukraine and before the Fed's aggressive rate-hiking cycle began in March 2022.
key_points: The Fed maintains a 2% longer-run inflation target measured by annual change in the PCE price index
The flexible average inflation targeting framework commits the Fed to achieving inflation moderately above 2% for some time following periods of persistently below-target inflation
The Committee judges the longer-run federal funds rate has declined relative to its historical average, increasing the likelihood of constraints from the effective lower bound
Maximum employment is defined as a broad-based, inclusive goal not directly measurable, assessed through shortfalls rather than fixed targets
The primary tool for adjusting monetary policy stance is changes in the target range for the federal funds rate
Data in the report reflects information available as of noon EST February 23, 2022 (with GDP data through February 24, 2022)
The Committee is prepared to use its full range of tools including asset purchases to achieve its mandate
why_it_matters: This quarterly Monetary Policy Report provides the official framework for Fed policy decision-making and was the baseline document before the Fed's most aggressive tightening cycle since the 1980s. Understanding the framework articulated here is essential context for interpreting subsequent policy actions.
macro_context: This report predates the Fed's pivot to aggressive tightening. It documents economic conditions from early 2022 when inflation was elevated (above 7% CPI at the time), employment was recovering, and the Fed was preparing to begin rate hikes. The report does not contain forward guidance on the pace of tightening that began in March 2022.
important_numbers: January 2012 (original adoption date of longer-run goals strategy)
